summary: Used asyncio+httpx (no hey/wrk installed). Ramped concurrency 1->5->10->25->50 for 10s each in heuristic mode (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY unset, sub-50ms expected in isolation). Peak success RPS=138.6 at c=10. Knee point at c=5 where p95 jumped from 9ms to 53ms (5.8x baseline). At c=50, p95=730ms, p99=1340ms — latency wall from SQLite write serialization. Zero true server errors (no 4xx other than 423, no 5xx). The 423 (frozen-account) responses (54% of all traffic) are an application-level side effect: the velocity rule fires when concurrent workers race the same user_id within a 5-minute window, freezing the account; load tester unfroze inline but the window between freeze and unfreeze shows up as 423s. Sequential baseline p50=4.95ms confirms the heuristic engine itself is extremely fast; all contention is in the SQLite write path.
brief_for_next: Sentinel heuristic-mode load test complete. Peak success RPS=138.6 at c=10; throughput degrades at c=25+ as SQLite write serialization dominates. Sequential p50=4.95ms (heuristic engine is near-zero CPU). Knee at c=5 (p95 5.8x baseline). At c=50: p50=215ms, p95=730ms, p99=1340ms — latency wall is SQLite single-writer lock under concurrent inserts. Zero true server errors across all 11,511 requests; the only non-200 codes were 423 (frozen-account velocity trigger, self-inflicted by the load test sharing user_ids). Critical bottlenecks: (1) SQLite in-memory store serializes all writes — replacing with PostgreSQL or even WAL-mode SQLite would unlock multi-writer concurrency; (2) single uvicorn worker with no thread pool means async overhead + DB waits compound linearly; (3) the velocity check + freeze mechanism is operationally hazardous at high load: 54% of all requests during the ramp returned 423 because concurrent workers triggered the 5-txn/5-min rule. In production, shared user_ids under load would cause false-positive mass freezes. DB is polluted with load-* transaction IDs; restart backend to reset in-memory SQLite.
